content:
Overall, your materials are strong and well-matched to the industry you’re entering. With a few refinements, they will be even more polished. Keep up the great work, and I look forward to seeing your final versions!

Best,
Professor Padavano

⸻

Letter for Alvin Gonzalez Naranjo

Dear Alvin,

I received your resume and cover letter, but unfortunately, it looks like the cover letter file may not have uploaded properly—it appears to be empty. Please check and resubmit it when you get a chance so I can give you feedback.

Regarding your resume, I noticed that it is missing key sections like an objective statement, work experience, and skills. Right now, it primarily consists of references, which is important but should not be the main focus of your resume. A resume should provide a clear picture of your background, education, work experience, and skills to show employers why you’re a strong candidate.

I recommend adding an objective at the top that states the type of position you’re seeking. Then, include your education details and list any work or volunteer experience you’ve had. Even if you don’t have a formal job history, you can highlight skills or relevant coursework. Also, organizing everything with proper formatting will help make it more readable.
context: From document: Feedback Letters for Resume & Cover Letters — chunk 2 (chunk 2/22)
